title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Given the heads of two sorted linked lists list1 and list2, merge them into a single sorted linked list. The merged list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists. Return the head of the merged linked list.

examples.explanation: Both lists are already sorted. Start with the first node of each list and compare their values. The smallest value (1 from list1) is added to the merged list first, then again the next 1 from list2, followed by 2 from list1, and so on.
Since list1 is empty, the merged list will simply be all the elements from list2 which contains a single node with value 0.
When both lists are empty, the merged result will also be empty as there are no nodes to merge.

constraints: The number of nodes in both lists is in the range [0, 50].
The value of each node is in the range [-100, 100].
hints: Consider using a dummy node to simplify merging logic.
Keep track of the last node added to the merged list.
Make sure to handle cases where one list is exhausted before the other.
You can use iterative or recursive methods for merging.
